<!-- GENERATED by scripts/sync-index.mjs — do not edit by hand. -->

# Architecture Decision Records

Every agent MUST read the ADRs relevant to its task before changing code. ADRs document **why**
decisions were made. Loading follows the contract in `.claude/rules/context-loading.md` and ADR-CORE-006:
always read `load: core`; read `conditional` when scope/triggers match; read `archival` only on
demand.

This index and `manifest.json` are generated from the ADR front-matter by `scripts/sync-index.mjs`.
Run `npm run governance:sync` after adding or editing an ADR; `npm run governance:check` enforces it.

## Format

```
# ADR-NNN: Title
- Status / Date / Metadata (scope, load, triggers, applies-to, supersedes)
- Context / Decision / Alternatives / Consequences / References
```

## Index

The **Layer** column is read straight off the id (`ADR-CORE-004`, `ADR-APP-026`, `ADR-PROJ-105`) —
it is not a second fact that could drift. A document in a lower layer may never cite one in a higher
layer; `governance:check` rejects it (ADR-CORE-033, ADR-CORE-034).

**Status is derived** (ADR-CORE-035): an ADR reads `superseded by X` when **X** declares
`supersedes: [it]` — including when X sits in a higher layer. The superseded file itself is never
edited, which is what lets a project retire an upstream decision it does not follow.

| ADR | Layer | Title | TL;DR | Status | Load |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| [ADR-APP-001](app-001-tauri-rust-react-stack.md) | app | Tauri 2 + Rust backend + React/TypeScript frontend | Desktop app on Tauri 2: Rust owns state, IO and the domain services, React+TS+Vite renders the HUD UI; small native binaries, one web UI for all OSes. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-APP-020](app-020-hud-design-system.md) | app | HUD design system — palette, chamfers, neon panels, no charting library | One HUD design system: palette in :root + Tailwind @theme tokens, clip-path chamfers, neon panels/buttons, Orbitron/Inter fonts; no charting library. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-APP-021](app-021-tauri-window-chrome.md) | app | Frameless Tauri window with custom HUD title bar | Frameless window; custom HUD TitleBar draggable via data-tauri-drag-region; window controls via the Tauri window API, drawn for all OSes. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-APP-023](app-023-cross-platform-release-signing.md) | app | Cross-platform release builds + optional macOS signing | tauri-action matrix builds macOS (arm+intel), Linux and Windows on tag/dispatch; macOS is signed+notarised when APPLE secrets are set, else unsigned. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-APP-025](app-025-logging-architecture.md) | app | Logging architecture — JSON file + ring buffer + live UI stream | tracing fans out to a pretty console layer, a rotating JSON file, and an in-memory ring buffer broadcast live to the UI log view; secret-free per ADR-CORE-011. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-APP-026](app-026-no-native-ui-primitives.md) | app | No native UI defaults — every control is a reusable HUD primitive | No stock controls: every control the user touches is a HUD primitive, whatever it is built on; native chrome suppressed or replaced; lint gate enforces it. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-APP-031](app-031-app-identity-ssot.md) | app | App-identity single source of truth (app.identity.json) with sync + drift gate | The app name/identifier lives once in app.identity.json; identity:sync propagates it everywhere; identity:check gates drift so it can't diverge. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-APP-032](app-032-crash-handling-mechanism.md) | app | Crash handling on this stack — the mechanism and the gate | ADR-CORE-037 on Tauri: panic hook before the builder, CrashBoundary + window handlers in the UI, a synchronous crash report, a native message box, a lint gate. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-CORE-002](core-002-best-solution-principle.md) | core | Always the best solution, never the easiest | Best, senior-level, production-grade — no shortcuts or unfinished leftovers; fix, never silently remove a feature (removal needs the owner's consent). | accepted | core |
| [ADR-CORE-003](core-003-repo-single-source-of-truth.md) | core | Repository is the single source of truth; memory is repo-resident | All decisions/rules/memory live in the repo; agent memory sits in .claude/memory and is symlinked into the user memory dir, so there is one truth and no drift. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-CORE-004](core-004-verify-first-no-guessing.md) | core | Verify first, never guess — every claim must be provable | Every claim must be verified and provable (source/test/measurement); no guessing; unverified items are marked 'open', never asserted as fact. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-CORE-005](core-005-reusability-policy.md) | core | Reusability policy — no structural duplication, shared single sources | Reuse over copy-paste; one source for cross-cutting concerns (ts-rs types, HUD theme, command wrappers) — no duplicated structure. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-CORE-006](core-006-context-budget-selective-loading.md) | core | Context budget — TLDR index + selective on-demand loading + compact-survival | Boot loads only CLAUDE.md + TLDR indexes + core docs; full ADRs/rules load on demand via tldr/triggers/applies-to; hooks force a reload after compact. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-CORE-007](core-007-generated-indexes-staleness-gate.md) | core | Generated indexes + staleness gate | One generator builds all indexes from doc front-matter; a content-hash scanner fails CI on drift. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-CORE-008](core-008-quality-pipeline.md) | core | Unified quality pipeline (check:all), pre-commit and CI | One check:all gate runs locally via pre-commit + pre-push and is the WHOLE gate; GitHub builds final releases at a tag and runs no push-/PR-triggered check. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-CORE-009](core-009-dependency-policy.md) | core | Dependency policy — latest stable everything, verified, lockfiles committed | Latest stable of every dependency (verified vs crates.io/npm), lockfiles committed — current versions minimise known CVEs and avoid an update cycle. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-CORE-010](core-010-testing-strategy.md) | core | Testing strategy — test-first (TDD), unit tests from the first module, contracts pinned both sides | Test-first (TDD): the failing test comes before the code; every module ships unit tests; IPC contracts pinned both sides; coverage gated in check:all. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-CORE-011](core-011-security-by-design.md) | core | Security by design — hardened Tauri, validated IPC, no secret leakage | Credentials in the OS keyring and never in the frontend; least-privilege capabilities, strict CSP, validated IPC inputs, app-data-only writes, HTTPS-only. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-CORE-022](core-022-agent-delegation-governance.md) | core | Agent delegation — model selection and subagent governance inheritance | Pick the right model per task (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku), brief each subagent with a task-fit senior persona, and make it inherit + never bypass project governance. | accepted | core |
| [ADR-CORE-024](core-024-versioning-and-build-channels.md) | core | Versioning (SSOT + SemVer) and dev/release build channels | package.json is the version SSOT (synced into Cargo.toml + Cargo.lock, gated in check:all); every change bumps SemVer; releasing is a separate, instructed act. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-CORE-030](core-030-portable-governance-core.md) | core | Portable governance core — versioned, hash-pinned, updatable across template forks | SUPERSEDED by ADR-CORE-033: two layers became N ordered layers. The hash pin, drift gate and self-updating governance:update are retained there. | superseded by ADR-CORE-033 | conditional |
| [ADR-CORE-032](core-032-config-layering-project-overlays.md) | core | Config layering — pinned core config, project overlays, explicit opt-out | Core config stays pinned; project-specific config is project-owned; eslint/knip take a project overlay; governance/opt-out.json opts a core path out of the pin. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-CORE-033](core-033-governance-layers-cascade.md) | core | Governance layers — an agnostic core, an application layer, and a publishing cascade | Governance is N ordered layers (core → app → project); a repo consumes upstream layers, may own one, and republishes the union to its own forks. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-CORE-034](core-034-layered-adr-identifiers.md) | core | The layer is part of the ADR id — ADR-<LAYER>-<NNN>, not a number block | ADR ids carry their layer (ADR-CORE-004, ADR-APP-026, ADR-PROJ-105); number blocks were a convention nobody could enforce, and were already violated. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-CORE-035](core-035-cross-layer-supersession.md) | core | Cross-layer supersession — declared in the superseding document, never in the superseded one | A project can retire an upstream decision by declaring `supersedes` in its OWN document; the pinned file is never touched. Direction: higher layer only. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-CORE-036](core-036-challenge-the-premise.md) | core | Challenge the premise — the agent is a critical partner, not an executor | Nothing guarded against the agent being agreeable: a wrong premise, executed perfectly, passes every gate. Object with evidence — and never invent an objection. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-CORE-037](core-037-no-silent-death.md) | core | No silent death — every entry point has a last-resort handler | Nothing governed the error nobody caught. A crash is permitted — a silent crash is not: every entry point reports before it dies. Survival needs proof. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-CORE-038](core-038-layered-briefing-names.md) | core | The layer is part of the briefing filename — <layer>-NNN-<slug>.md, not a number range | Briefings are named <layer>-NNN-<slug>.md (core-001-…, app-001-…); number ranges were unchecked prose, and a collision aborts a consumer's governance:update. | accepted | conditional |
| [ADR-PROJ-001](project/proj-001-video-domain-architecture.md) | proj | Video domain architecture — ffmpeg-backed pipeline, internal player, non-destructive output, i18n | ffmpeg/ffprobe run as external processes (discovered or in-app installed); job queue, internal <video> player, strictly non-destructive output, de/en UI. | accepted | conditional |
